**Remix with Replit link \-** https://replit.com/join/lewyoinxbfpusqd-russellcaseysmi

And select REMIX

**![][image1]**

And then Title your new Remix, then click the blue “Remix App” button

username: admin@admin.com  
password: admin12345678

**And download the zip file**

**Next, upload the zip file to your Replit account via Zip**

**Next, download the database**

**Default Credentials**  
username: admin@admin.com  
password: admin12345678

**Longest and hardest way to clone this application**

**Here is the Bigfoot Blueprint software github repo: (Copy This URL for Replit)**  
[https://github.com/djdangeruss/Bigfoot-Blueprint-Directories-Pro.git](https://github.com/djdangeruss/bigfoot-blueprint-directories.git)

**Prompt to Get Your App Restarted for Onboarding:**

“Set up this pnpm monorepo so it runs in the preview pane. The project has an Express API backend and a React/Vite frontend. Please: install dependencies, push the database schema, configure the workflow to start both servers, set up the Gemini AI integration, fix the canvas iframe so the app is visible.

That **"Directory Master" dropdown** appeared automatically — no specific prompt was needed to trigger it. Replit detected the artifacts/ folder structure in the monorepo and automatically registered each subfolder as a named artifact:

* artifacts/directory-master → **Directory Master** (web)  
* artifacts/api-server → **API Server** (api)  
* artifacts/mockup-sandbox → **Canvas** (design)

That's built into how Replit handles monorepos with an artifacts/ directory — it shows up in the Preview dropdown without any prompting.

What did require a prompt was fixing the white screen. The canvas iframe shape had an empty URL and needed to be pointed at the running app. The fix was:  
*"I clicked refresh and I still see nothing. This is an issue, please resolve it."*  
That triggered the investigation where I found the canvas iframe had url: "" and updated it to your app's live URL. Once that was set, the preview loaded correctly.  
Can you go ahead and set the token to be set up where I don't have to bother about getting it confirmed when I click install directory?  
Can you do a quick refresh to see an audit log of why this error would still be occurring? I believe the root cause is clear from the logs:

* **artifacts/api-server: API Server** (port 8080\) has the **old build** — it returns 404 for /api/setup/token because it hasn't been rebuilt since my code change  
* The **Directory Master artifact** proxies /api to port 8080 (not port 3000\)  
* So the token auto-fetch silently fails → form submits with an empty token → 403  
* update the port to :5000 → :80

You will need to restart the artifact API server workflow so it rebuilds.”

**Prompt to Get Your App Restarted for Live Canvas Preview:**  
“Check the canvas iframe state to see if the URL needs to be fixed. My preview isn’t showing on the display. Make sure The canvas iframe is live and the app is rendering correctly in the preview pane.   
Canvas iframe → pointed at your live dev URL  
Frontend (port 5000\) → setup wizard with no errors  
API (port 8080\) → token auto-fetched, proxy”

**Troubleshooting:**  
“Check my live preview right now as I am having a white screen that I need you to resolve. Please update this internally.”

**Default Credentials**  
username: admin@admin.com  
password: admin1234578

**Here is a copy of the database to get started:** (Download and upload to Replit’s Agent)  
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1IaWWSVeYWmXS\_tLQD4Pu2MyTwXdZpp\_o/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1IaWWSVeYWmXS_tLQD4Pu2MyTwXdZpp_o/view?usp=sharing)

**Prompt to send with this database file:**  
“Go ahead and import this database and populate my application.”

**Final White Screen Troubleshooting:**  
I have a pnpm monorepo called "Directory Master" — a self-hosted white-label  
directory website builder. The workspace has these packages:  
artifacts/directory-master → React \+ Vite frontend (dev port 5000\)  
artifacts/api-server → Express 5 API backend (dev port 8080\)  
lib/db → Drizzle ORM \+ PostgreSQL schema  
Please do ALL of the following in order:  
1\. INSTALL DEPENDENCIES  
Run: pnpm install  
2\. PUSH THE DATABASE SCHEMA  
Run: pnpm \--filter @workspace/db run push  
3\. SET ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES  
Set these in \[userenv.shared\] (not secrets):  
PORT \= 5000  
BASE\_PATH \= /  
API\_PORT \= 8080  
4\. PROVISION GEMINI AI INTEGRATION  
Install the Replit blueprint: javascript\_gemini\_ai\_integrations==2.0.0  
This sets AI\_INTEGRATIONS\_GEMINI\_BASE\_URL and AI\_INTEGRATIONS\_GEMINI\_API\_KEY automatically.  
5\. CONFIGURE VITE PROXY  
In artifacts/directory-master/vite.config.ts, add a server.proxy block so  
that /api and /sitemap.xml forward to http://localhost:8080 (the API port).  
This prevents 404s on API calls during development.  
6\. FIX THE SETUP WIZARD (skip manual token step)  
a) Add a GET /api/setup/token endpoint to the API server. It must:  
\- Only work when the app is NOT yet installed (installed=false in DB)  
\- Return { token: "\<current setup token\>" } as JSON  
\- Return 404 if already installed  
b) In the frontend setup page (src/pages/setup.tsx):  
\- Default the wizard step to 2 (Admin Account), not 1 (Token)  
\- On mount, auto-fetch GET /api/setup/token and apply it silently  
\- Move all useEffect hooks BEFORE any early-return statements to  
avoid React hooks-order violations  
7\. FIX EXPRESS 5 WILDCARD ROUTE SYNTAX  
Express 5 uses path-to-regexp v8, which no longer accepts bare \* wildcards.  
Find every route that uses "\*" or "\*paramName" and update to "{\*paramName}".  
Common locations:  
\- artifacts/api-server/src/routes/storage.ts:  
"/storage/public-objects/\*filePath" → "/storage/public-objects/{\*filePath}"  
"/storage/objects/\*path" → "/storage/objects/{\*path}"  
\- artifacts/api-server/src/app.ts (catch-all SPA fallback):  
app.get("\*", ...) → app.get("/{\*wildcard}", ...)  
8\. CONFIGURE WORKFLOWS  
Create two workflows:  
a) "API Server"  
command: PORT=$API\_PORT pnpm \--filter @workspace/api-server run dev  
waitForPort: 8080  
outputType: console  
b) "Start application"  
command: PORT=5000 BASE\_PATH=/ pnpm \--filter @workspace/directory-master run dev  
waitForPort: 5000  
outputType: webview ← MUST be 5000 for Replit webview  
9\. UPDATE CANVAS IFRAME  
After both workflows are running, update the canvas iframe shape  
(id: artifact:v3:default-start-application) so its url points at the  
live Replit dev domain (https://\<repl-slug\>.janeway.replit.dev) and  
set state to "live".  
10\. CONFIGURE PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT  
deploymentTarget: autoscale (Cloud Run)  
build: pnpm install \--frozen-lockfile && pnpm \--filter @workspace/db run push  
&& PORT=5000 BASE\_PATH=/ pnpm \--filter @workspace/directory-master run build  
&& pnpm \--filter @workspace/api-server run build  
run: NODE\_ENV=production PORT=8080 node \--enable-source-maps  
artifacts/api-server/dist/index.mjs  
IMPORTANT: Production must use PORT=8080. Cloud Run's health probe checks  
port 8080 by default. If PORT=5000 is used, the health check times out and  
the deployment fails even though the build succeeds.  
11\. SERVE STATIC FILES IN PRODUCTION  
In artifacts/api-server/src/app.ts, add this block AFTER app.use("/api", router):  
if (process.env.NODE\_ENV \=== "production") {  
const staticDir \= path.resolve(\_\_dirname,  
"../../directory-master/dist/public");  
app.use(express.static(staticDir));  
app.get("/{\*wildcard}", (\_req, res) \=\> {  
res.sendFile(path.join(staticDir, "index.html"));  
});  
}  
This makes the single API server serve both the REST API and the React SPA  
in production — no separate frontend server needed.  
After completing all steps, take a screenshot to verify the app is visible in  
the preview pane showing the setup wizard or the homepage (if already installed).

I just ran this on the new window and I'm getting this error in console, GET net::ERR\_HTTP\_RESPONSE\_CODE\_FAILURE 502 (Bad Gateway)

**Final Troubleshooting**  
Exit the app and Open it Again
